summary.low_unstructured: This code defines a React component that displays a stock price line chart using Recharts.
summary.title: StockChart Component for Rendering Stock Price Line Charts
summary.medium_structured:
• Defines StockChart, a React component for visualizing stock prices
• Checks if input data is valid before rendering
• Extracts company name and ticker from data
• Prepares chart data with date and closing price
• Renders a responsive line chart with axes, tooltip, and legend
summary.high_unstructured: The provided code defines a StockChart React component that renders a responsive line chart of stock closing prices over time using the Recharts library. It first validates the input data, extracting the company name and ticker symbol for display. The component transforms the raw data into a format suitable for Recharts, mapping each entry to an object with date and price fields. The chart includes axes, tooltips, a legend, and a styled line representing the stock's price trend, and it is displayed only if valid data is provided.
summary.low_structured:
• React component for stock price chart
• Uses Recharts library
• Displays data if available
summary.medium_unstructured: This code implements a React component called StockChart that visualizes stock price data as a line chart using the Recharts library. It checks for valid data input, extracts relevant company and ticker information, and formats the data for charting. The chart displays the stock's closing prices over time with axes, tooltips, and a legend.
summary.high_structured:
• Implements StockChart, a React functional component for displaying stock price trends
• Data validation:
  ◦ Checks if the data prop and its nested data array are present and non-empty
  ◦ Returns null if data is invalid, preventing rendering
• Extracts company name and ticker symbol from the input data for chart labeling
• Prepares chartData array:
  ◦ Maps each data point to an object with 'date' and 'price' (from 'close') fields
• Renders a styled container with:
  ◦ A heading showing company and ticker
  ◦ A ResponsiveContainer holding a LineChart
    ◦ LineChart uses chartData and custom margins
    ◦ Includes XAxis (date), YAxis (auto domain), Tooltip, and Legend
    ◦ Plots a monotone Line for 'price' with a specific color and no dots
• Utilizes Recharts components for interactive and responsive charting

summary.title: Express Server Providing Stock Data API with Mock and Real Data Options
summary.medium_structured:
• Initializes an Express server with CORS and JSON middleware
• Defines a '/api/hello' endpoint returning a greeting
• Provides a '/api/stock/:symbol' endpoint
  ◦ Returns mock stock data by default
  ◦ Can fetch real data from Yahoo Finance if USE_MOCK is false
• Listens on port 3000 and logs server status
summary.high_unstructured: This code creates an Express server that listens on port 3000 and supports CORS and JSON request bodies. It defines a '/api/hello' endpoint for a simple greeting and a '/api/stock/:symbol' endpoint that, by default, returns generated mock stock data for a given symbol, simulating daily prices and volumes for a year (excluding weekends). If the USE_MOCK flag is set to false, the endpoint fetches real historical stock data from Yahoo Finance, formats it, and returns it in a consistent structure. The implementation includes error handling for failed data fetches and logs server activity to the console.
summary.low_structured:
• Sets up an Express server
• Provides endpoints for stock data and a hello message
• Uses mock data for stock info by default
summary.medium_unstructured: The code initializes an Express server with CORS and JSON parsing enabled, exposing endpoints for a hello message and stock data retrieval. By default, it serves mock stock data for a given symbol, but it can also fetch real data from Yahoo Finance if configured. The server listens on port 3000 and handles errors gracefully.
summary.high_structured:
• Sets up an Express server with CORS and JSON body parsing
• Defines endpoints:
  ◦ '/api/hello': returns a static greeting message
  ◦ '/api/stock/:symbol': returns stock data for the requested symbol
    ◦ By default (USE_MOCK = true), generates mock data for 1 year (excluding weekends) with random prices and volumes
    ◦ If USE_MOCK is false, fetches real daily stock data from Yahoo Finance starting from 2024-01-01
    ◦ Formats both mock and real data into a consistent JSON structure with ticker, name, and data array
• Handles errors by logging and sending a 500 response if stock data retrieval fails
• Starts the server on port 3000 and logs the running status

The server now includes a currentPrice field in both mock and real stock data responses, listens on port 3000, and handles errors gracefully.
new_summary.high_structured:
• Sets up an Express server with CORS and JSON body parsing
• Defines endpoints:
  ◦ '/api/hello': returns a static greeting message
  ◦ '/api/stock/:symbol': returns stock data for the requested symbol
    ◦ By default (USE_MOCK = true), generates mock data for 1 year (excluding weekends) with random prices and volumes, now also including a currentPrice field (latest closing price)
    ◦ If USE_MOCK is false, fetches real daily stock data from Yahoo Finance starting from 2024-01-01, and includes currentPrice in the response
    ◦ Formats both mock and real data into a consistent JSON structure with ticker, name, data array, and currentPrice
• Handles errors by logging and sending a 500 response if stock data retrieval fails
• Starts the server on port 3000 and logs the running status

data.summary: This code creates an Express server that listens on port 3000 and supports CORS and JSON request bodies. It defines a '/api/hello' endpoint for a simple greeting and a '/api/stock/:symbol' endpoint that, by default, returns generated mock stock data for a given symbol, simulating daily prices and volumes for a year (excluding weekends), and now also includes a currentPrice field representing the latest closing price. If the USE_MOCK flag is set to false, the endpoint fetches real historical stock data from Yahoo Finance, formats it, and returns it in a consistent structure that now contains currentPrice as well. The implementation includes error handling for failed data fetches and logs server activity to the console.

• Formats dates for display and highlights the latest closing price
• Displays company name and ticker above the chart
summary.high_unstructured: This code defines a StockChart React component that visualizes stock price data as a responsive line chart using the Recharts library. It first validates the input data, extracts the company name and ticker, and transforms the raw data into a format suitable for charting. The component customizes the X-axis to display months, and draws a line for closing prices, with a red dashed reference line marking the latest closing price. Additional features include tooltips, a legend, and responsive sizing, all wrapped in a styled container with the company and ticker displayed as a heading.

summary.medium_unstructured: The code implements a React component that renders a line chart of stock prices using the Recharts library. It formats and displays the company's name, ticker, and price data, and highlights the latest closing price with a reference line. The chart is responsive and includes axes, tooltips, and a legend for better readability.
summary.high_structured:
• Implements a StockChart React component for visualizing stock price data
• Data Handling:
  ◦ Validates input data and extracts company name and ticker
  ◦ Transforms raw data into objects with date and close price
• Chart Configuration:
  ◦ Uses Recharts components: LineChart, XAxis, YAxis, Tooltip, Legend, ResponsiveContainer, ReferenceLine
  ◦ X-axis labels are formatted to show month abbreviations
  ◦ Plots a monotone line for closing prices, with dots disabled for clarity
• Highlights the latest closing price with a red dashed ReferenceLine
• Renders the chart responsively and displays the company name and ticker above the chart

          data={chartData}
          margin={{ top: 10, right: 30, left: 0, bottom: 0 }}

          <YAxis domain={["auto", "auto"]} />
          <Tooltip />
          <Legend />
          <Line type="monotone" dataKey="price" stroke="#8884d8" dot={false} />

              strokeDasharray="5 5"
